title: ThoughtProof Sentinel Canonical Verdict Body (v1)
description: Byte-deterministic (JCS / RFC 8785) projection of a ThoughtProof Sentinel verify response. This is the exact artifact ThoughtProof hands to invinoveritas for anchoring. A third party can recompute sha256 over the JCS serialization of these fields and match the anchored body_hash without trusting either party. Source of truth: thoughtproof-sentinel/src/canonical-verdict.ts.

artifactSchema.description: Schema identity. Distinguishes Sentinel verdicts from plv.verdict.canonical.v1 etc.

verificationId.description: Stable Sentinel verification id (matches response.id). Use as the attributed key.

apiVersion.description: Sentinel API body-shape version. Bump if the canonical body shape changes.

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence as a 0-100 integer. NOTE: the live API response uses a 0-1 float; the canonical body uses 0-100 int (round(response.confidence * 100), clamped).

objections.description: Material objections, each mapped deterministically as "${step_id}: ${reasoning}". May be empty.

reasoning.description: Human-readable rationale. Empty string when absent (never null).

secondary.description: Second evaluator if the cascade escalated (e.g. serv-swift). OMITTED ENTIRELY for solo tiers — never null.

gate.description: Deterministic-gate result. Present ONLY for mode=action_authorization with a mandate. OMITTED ENTIRELY otherwise — never null (JCS treats absent vs null distinctly).

$comment: JCS DETERMINISM RULE (load-bearing for hash recomputability): optional fields (models.secondary, gate) are omitted entirely when absent, never set to null. Serialize via RFC 8785 JCS, then sha256. See canonical.json for a matching sample and its hash in README.
